title: impatiens grey-wilsonii
intro: Impatiens grey-wilsonii, also known as the Grey-Wilson's jewelweed, is a species of flowering plant in the balsam family. The plant is primarily used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of skin irritations, such as eczema and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Impatiens grey-wilsonii include reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or mixing the plant's sap with carrier oils to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
